prompt: I need to decide whether to switch our customer support platform from Zendesk to Intercom. We've been on Zendesk for 3 years, have about 2,000 tickets/month, 8 support agents. The main drivers: Intercom is 30% cheaper at our tier, our team says the UI is better, and it has a product tours feature we want. Migration would take about 2 months and $15k in engineering time. We've already done a 2-week trial with Intercom. Can you stress-test this decision?

assertions.text: The report correctly identifies this as a two-way door (reversible) decision
The report applies lighter/faster analysis appropriate for a two-way door, rather than maximum depth
The report mentions migration risk, data loss, or disruption to support operations during transition
The report identifies hidden costs beyond the stated $15k (e.g., retraining, productivity dip, integration rebuilding)
The report includes external market context about Zendesk vs Intercom based on research (not just restating user's input)
